<think> I recall that the question is asking about an Ohio State Buckeyes football player who challenged the NFL's draft eligibility rules. I think of several notable players from Ohio State, but one name that stands out is Maurice Clarett. He was a running back who played for Ohio State from 2002 to 2004. I remember that he was involved in a high-profile case regarding the NFL's draft eligibility rules. In 2005, Clarett attempted to enter the NFL draft, but the NFL challenged his eligibility due to the fact that he was only two years removed from high school. He eventually lost his case and was not allowed to enter the draft. This event led to changes in the NFL's draft eligibility rules. Therefore, I believe Maurice Clarett is the player who challenged the NFL's draft eligibility rules. </think>
<answer> Maurice Clarett </answer>
